title: That bad feeling
wikitext:
[]: That bad feeling...             Quote Post by Darius » Sun Aug 20, 2006 6:52 pm Hey guys...After seeing all the wide ranges of omakes and fan stuff and all this other Eva stuff...I got this feeling...It's a very bad feeling. not like a premonition feeling..but like a state of mind...I think Its the feeling that you'd want to get all of this Eva stuff...or any stuff you like in particular...but you just know you can't get it.. That is the feeling I hate and that I have at the moment.. Anyone else got that feeling or had it?

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Sun Aug 20, 2006 7:58 pm 24/7/365

[]:             Quote Post by Darius » Sun Aug 20, 2006 8:11 pm Yes..That must suck..This is a baaaaaad feeling. I guess the only cure is to actually get everything..

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Aug 20, 2006 9:54 pm I've never had that feeling. All things exist somewhere, and if you want them badly enough, you can get them.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sun Aug 20, 2006 10:01 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: I've never had that feeling. All things exist somewhere, and if you want them badly enough, you can get them.

[]: Re: That bad feeling...             Quote Post by Faußtin » Mon Aug 21, 2006 4:22 am Darius wrote: Hey guys...After seeing all the wide ranges of omakes and fan stuff and all this other Eva stuff...I got this feeling...It's a very bad feeling. not like a premonition feeling..but like a state of mind...I think Its the feeling that you'd want to get all of this Eva stuff...or any stuff you like in particular...but you just know you can't get it.. That is the feeling I hate and that I have at the moment.. Anyone else got that feeling or had it?

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Mon Aug 21, 2006 5:24 am Hexon.Arq wrote: I've never had that feeling. All things exist somewhere, and if you want them badly enough, you can get them.

[]:             Quote Post by Bardiel » Mon Aug 21, 2006 6:57 am I know exactly what you mean. Ussualy I get this feeling when I read about al those books and things I've never heard about. I always get the feeling I'm trying to reach an inpossible goal.

[]:             Quote Post by Darius » Mon Aug 21, 2006 9:32 am Ya. It's just sooo annoying....Well, we have to always look to the future and hop we can get em.. Heh " Gotta Catch em' All!"

[]:             Quote Post by the3rdchildren » Mon Aug 21, 2006 9:52 am SEELE 08 wrote: Hexon.Arq wrote: I've never had that feeling. All things exist somewhere, and if you want them badly enough, you can get them.

[]:             Quote Post by Darius » Mon Aug 21, 2006 10:12 am the3rdchildren wrote: Sadly I've never come across nor heard of an Keel and SEELE action figure playtime set....though I still have plans to someday make a "Vlad" plushie.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Mon Aug 21, 2006 11:21 am It's way more fun wanting it, and then actually finding a way to get your hands on it. Way more fun than having it sitting around on a shelf somewhere, with you going; "...That's it?" The chase is a hundred times better than the catch when it comes to collecting memorabilia.

[]:             Quote Post by the3rdchildren » Mon Aug 21, 2006 11:56 am Kaysow wrote: It's way more fun wanting it, and then actually finding a way to get your hands on it. Way more fun than having it sitting around on a shelf somewhere, with you going; "...That's it?" The chase is a hundred times better than the catch when it comes to collecting memorabilia.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Mon Aug 21, 2006 12:47 pm An elaboration; Think of what you'd do if you actually owned that particular figurine or whatever. I bought an Asuka once, and now it's stuffed in a drawer somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Mon Aug 21, 2006 12:55 pm Darius wrote: the3rdchildren wrote: Sadly I've never come across nor heard of an Keel and SEELE action figure playtime set....though I still have plans to someday make a "Vlad" plushie.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Mon Aug 21, 2006 3:56 pm Can't say that I ever get that feeling about something like Eva merchandise. Most of that junk is just simple wants and not worth worrying or fretting over. I may like it but if it cost more than I am willing to spend I got no problem passing on it.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Thu Aug 24, 2006 12:29 am LOL, is there anything that they don't make Kubrick figures for? :P

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Aug 24, 2006 1:23 am Kaysow wrote: An elaboration; Think of what you'd do if you actually owned that particular figurine or whatever. I bought an Asuka once, and now it's stuffed in a drawer somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Thu Aug 24, 2006 1:35 am Today I started painting my Unit 02 model kit. It's coming out quite nicely, and hopefully I'll have assembled it. But what's truly frightening was that I was actually able to get eyelashes onto a figurine that's barely more than 2 inches tall. :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by Xanatose » Thu Aug 24, 2006 1:37 am I own absolutely no Eva related merchandise, and no merchandise of pop culture in general, save a few old CDs (the majority of which are actually symphonic music.) The reason is that I have a complete distaste for consumer culture. A healthy way to foster this and avoid spending your money on useless doo-hickies is to ask yourself, quite frankly, three reasons why you don't need whatever interests you at the moment. If you can come up with three, that's usually more than enough to not get something. Incidentally, with the glories of the interweb, you don't really have to buy anything anymore. Just know where to look (and where not to ask... silly anti-pirate forums ;>_>)
